items.title: HUD opens $10M in extra admin fees for HUD-VASH voucher leasing; applications due April 14, 2026
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-01 (Revised) makes $10 million available in additional administrative fees for public housing agencies (PHAs) that currently administer HUD-VASH to help lease HUD-VASH vouchers. PHAs must submit applications by midnight on April 14, 2026 (in the PHA’s time zone), and funds are allocated based on the number of HUD-VASH vouchers a PHA administers and how many PHAs apply.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HUD proposes time limits (as short as 2 years) and work requirements (up to 40 hours/week) for rental aid
items.summary: HUD proposed a rule that would let local housing authorities and private landlords impose time limits on rental assistance, potentially as short as two years, and require up to 40 hours of work per week as a condition of receiving aid. The proposal would exempt elderly and disabled recipients and would be open for public comment for 60 days.

items.title: USDA sets FY2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash benefit amounts; states must offer at least one non-fresh option by April 2026
items.summary: USDA set FY 2026 monthly WIC cash-value voucher/benefit (CVV/B) amounts at $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/partially breastfeeding participants. USDA also noted that WIC food package changes require state agencies to authorize at least one form of fruits and vegetables beyond fresh (frozen, canned, and/or dried) by April 2026.

items.title: Social Security announces 2.8% COLA for 2026 affecting Social Security and SSI payments
items.summary: The Social Security Administration announced a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment (COLA) for 2026 affecting more than 75 million people receiving Social Security and SSI. The increase takes effect in January for Social Security recipients and on December 31, 2025 for nearly 7.5 million SSI beneficiaries.

items.title: Federal FAFSA deadline for 2025–26 aid is June 30, 2026 (11:59 p.m. CT); corrections due Sept. 12, 2026
items.summary: Federal Student Aid says students must submit the 2025–26 FAFSA by 11:59 p.m. CT on June 30, 2026 to be considered for federal student aid for that award year. Corrections or updates must be submitted by 11:59 p.m. CT on Sept. 12, 2026.

items.title: HHS releases about $3.7B in LIHEAP funding to states (about 90% of FY2026 allocation)
items.summary: HHS’s Administration for Children and Families released about $3.6 billion in regular LIHEAP block grant funding plus an additional $100 million in supplemental Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act funding to states. The release represented about 90% of the $4.015 billion allocated for LIHEAP for FY 2026 under a continuing resolution.

items.title: HUD reissues PIH Notice 2026-01 with HUD-VASH admin fee guidance (revised March 25, 2026)
items.summary: HUD’s PIH Notice 2026-01 (Revised) provides guidance on requesting and using HUD-VASH administrative fee funding, including eligible uses like landlord recruitment, housing search assistance, landlord/tenant mediation, owner incentives, and security deposits (subject to program rules). The revised notice was issued March 25, 2026 and references earlier HUD guidance on voucher funding provisions and HUD-VASH admin fees.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: PATH Act delay: EITC/ACTC refunds typically held until mid-February; IRS updates expected starting Feb. 17, 2026
items.summary: Under the PATH Act, refunds claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) or Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) are held until after mid-February to allow extra verification. Jackson Hewitt notes that in 2026 the IRS lifts the hold on Feb. 16 (Presidents’ Day), begins processing Feb. 17, and most refunds are expected to be deposited by March 6, 2026.

items.title: Federal policy groups summarize Medicaid work requirement framework under 2025 budget law (implementation targeted for 2027)
items.summary: Center for Health Care Strategies and other policy organizations summarize that the 2025 budget reconciliation law established Medicaid work requirements (often 80 hours per month) for certain adults ages 19–64, with states required to implement by January 1, 2027 (with possible earlier start dates). The summaries highlight exemptions and implementation timelines that states and enrollees will need to track.

items.title: LIHEAP FY2026 funding totals reported at about $4.015B (with early-season funding releases emphasized)
items.summary: A Public Power report on LIHEAP funding said the FY2026 allocation totaled about $4.015 billion and described an HHS/ACF release of roughly $3.7 billion (regular plus supplemental funds) to states. The report emphasized that the early release represented about 90% of annual funding, which can matter for state program start-up and winter heating/cooling assistance.
